items.text: Establish and document a lawful basis for each data processing activity
Display a clear, pre-action consent banner with granular opt-in choices
Ensure no tracking cookies or scripts fire before consent is obtained
Provide a comprehensive, accessible privacy policy
Implement a mechanism for data subject access requests (DSARs)
Support the right to erasure (right to be forgotten)
Support data portability — export user data in machine-readable format
Maintain a Record of Processing Activities (ROPA)
Conduct Data Protection Impact Assessments (DPIAs) for high-risk processing
Appoint a Data Protection Officer (DPO) if required
Establish a 72-hour data breach notification procedure
Ensure all third-party processors have signed Data Processing Agreements
Implement cross-border data transfer safeguards (SCCs, adequacy decisions)
Store consent records with timestamps, versions, and user choices
Obtain informed consent before placing non-essential cookies or trackers
Provide clear information about each cookie's purpose before consent
Allow users to refuse non-essential cookies without loss of access
Ensure reject option is equally prominent as accept (per enforcement guidance)
Respect cookie consent on a per-purpose basis (analytics, advertising, functional)
Do not use cookie walls that force consent as a condition of access
Maintain a record of cookies and trackers deployed on the site
Ensure confidentiality of electronic communications data
Obtain explicit end-user consent before combining data across gatekeeper services
Provide a clear, separate consent flow for cross-service data linking
Allow users to decline cross-service profiling without losing core functionality
Support consent signals for gatekeeper platforms (Google, Apple, Meta, etc.)
Audit data flows to identify where gatekeeper consent requirements apply
Document compliance measures for each designated gatekeeper interaction
Conduct an inventory of all AI systems used in your organization
Classify each AI system by risk level (unacceptable, high, limited, minimal)
Eliminate any AI systems classified as unacceptable risk
Implement conformity assessments for high-risk AI systems
Ensure transparency obligations for AI systems interacting with individuals
Maintain technical documentation and logging for high-risk AI systems
Implement human oversight mechanisms for high-risk AI systems
Disclose when content is AI-generated (deepfakes, synthetic text)
Register high-risk AI systems in the EU public database
Comply with UK GDPR data protection principles (lawfulness, fairness, transparency)
Appoint a UK representative if processing UK residents' data from abroad
Use UK-specific International Data Transfer Agreement (IDTA) for transfers
Register with the ICO if required and pay the data protection fee
Comply with DUAA reforms: updated lawful bases and recognized legitimate interests
Follow updated DUAA cookie rules and consent requirements
Support data subject rights: access, rectification, erasure, portability
Implement a 72-hour breach notification process to the ICO
Inform users about data collection purposes before processing begins
Obtain explicit consent for processing sensitive personal data
Maintain a register of processing activities
Conduct DPIAs for high-risk data processing
Ensure cross-border transfers comply with Swiss adequacy requirements
Appoint a representative in Switzerland if processing from abroad
Implement a 72-hour breach notification process to FDPIC
Provide data subject rights: access, rectification, deletion, portability
Define and document a legal basis for each processing activity
Obtain free, informed, and unambiguous consent where consent is the legal basis
Display a Portuguese-language consent banner for Brazilian users
Appoint a Data Protection Officer (Encarregado)
Provide data subject rights: confirmation, access, correction, anonymization, deletion
Allow data portability to another service provider
Maintain processing records and submit to ANPD upon request
Implement an incident response plan for data breaches
Ensure international data transfers have adequate safeguards
Register with the Information Regulator if required
Appoint an Information Officer and register with the Regulator
Obtain consent or identify another lawful condition for processing
Provide notice to data subjects about the purpose of collection
Implement appropriate security safeguards for personal information
Support data subject rights: access, correction, deletion, objection
Restrict cross-border transfers to countries with adequate protection
Notify the Regulator and data subjects of security compromises
Obtain meaningful consent before collecting, using, or disclosing personal information
Limit collection to what is necessary for identified purposes
Provide clear notice of purposes at or before the time of collection
Allow individuals to access and correct their personal information
Appoint a Privacy Officer responsible for compliance
Implement safeguards appropriate to the sensitivity of the data
Report breaches of security safeguards to the OPC and affected individuals
Retain personal information only as long as necessary for the identified purpose
Obtain separate consent for processing sensitive personal information
Conduct a Personal Information Protection Impact Assessment for high-risk processing
Store personal information of Chinese residents within China or pass security assessment
Appoint a personal information protection officer if processing thresholds are met
Provide data subject rights: access, correction, deletion, portability, withdrawal of consent
Use standard contracts, certification, or security assessment for cross-border transfers
Maintain processing records for at least three years
Notify authorities and affected individuals in case of a data breach
Obtain verifiable consent before processing digital personal data
Provide notice of purpose, categories of data, and rights at the point of collection
Obtain verifiable parental consent before processing children's data
Support data principal rights: access, correction, erasure, grievance redressal
Implement appropriate security safeguards for personal data
Report data breaches to the Data Protection Board and affected data principals
Delete personal data once the purpose is fulfilled and retention period expires
Ensure cross-border transfers comply with government-notified restrictions
Register as a Consent Manager if operating as a third-party consent platform
Specify and publicly announce the purpose of use of personal information
Obtain consent before providing personal data to third parties
Implement security controls appropriate to the data handled
Respond to individual requests for disclosure, correction, and cessation of use
Report data breaches to the PPC and affected individuals
Ensure overseas data transfers meet APPI adequacy or consent requirements
Maintain records of third-party data provision and receipt
Obtain consent for collection, use, and third-party provision of personal data
Appoint a Chief Privacy Officer (CPO)
Conduct a Privacy Impact Assessment for high-risk processing
Support data subject rights: access, correction, deletion, suspension of processing
Implement technical and organizational security measures
Notify PIPC and affected individuals within 72 hours of a data breach
Comply with cross-border transfer restrictions and consent or safeguard requirements
Appoint a local representative if processing from outside South Korea

Provide a privacy notice with purpose, legal basis, and retention period
Appoint a Data Protection Officer if required

Implement technical and organizational security measures
Notify data subjects and authorities within 72 hours of a breach
Ensure cross-border transfers meet adequacy or safeguard requirements
Appoint a Data Protection Officer if required by processing scale
Obtain consent before collecting, using, or disclosing personal data
Notify individuals of purposes before or at the time of collection
Appoint a Data Protection Officer
Implement reasonable security measures to protect personal data
Support access and correction requests from individuals
Comply with data breach notification requirements to PDPC and individuals
Ensure cross-border transfers maintain comparable protection levels
Retain personal data only as long as necessary for business or legal purposes
Appoint a Data Protection Officer (mandatory per 2024 amendments)
Obtain consent before processing personal data

Implement data breach notification procedures (per 2024 amendments)
Support data portability rights (per 2024 amendments)
Implement security safeguards appropriate to the data processed
Ensure cross-border transfers comply with adequacy requirements
Comply with the Australian Privacy Principles (APPs) for handling personal information

Notify individuals about the collection of their personal information and its purpose

Register as a CMP with IAB Europe and obtain CMP ID
Implement IAB TCF 2.3 CMP API (__tcfapi) on all pages

Integrate Google Consent Mode v2 via gtag.js or GTM
Set default consent states (denied) before any Google tags load
Map CMP consent categories to Google's consent types (ad_storage, analytics_storage, ad_user_data, ad_personalization)

Implement Amazon Ads consent signal API on your website
Map CMP consent categories to Amazon's signal parameters
Send consent status before any Amazon ad tags fire

Test signal delivery in Amazon Ads console sandbox

Add Microsoft UET consent mode parameter to your UET tag
Set default consent state to denied before UET tag loads
